definitions.text: Away from the inside or centre.
examples: The magician tapped the hat, and a rabbit jumped out.
There was a hole in the bucket, and all the water leaked out.

definitions.text: Away from, or at a distance from, some point of reference or focus.
examples: Once they had landed, the commandos quickly spread out along the beach.
For six hours the tide flows out, then for six hours it flows in.

definitions.text: Specifically, away from home or one's usual place.
From the inside to the outside of; out of. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: throw it out the window; get it out your mind
We are gonna get evicted. —If your fat butt fits out the door, that is.

definitions.text: A means of exit, escape, reprieve, etc.
examples: They wrote the law to give those organizations an out.

definitions.text: A state in which a member of the batting team is removed from play due to the application of various rules of the game such as striking out, hitting a fly ball which is caught by the fielding team before bouncing, etc.
To come or go out; to get out or away; to become public, revealed, or apparent.
Not inside or within a place, especially a place that someone or something was formerly inside or is customarily inside:
examples: I worked away cleaning the U-bend until all the gunge was out.
This building is unsafe. Keep out!

definitions.text: Not at home, or not at one's office or place of employment.
examples: I'm sorry, Mr Smith is out at the moment.

definitions.text: Not in jail, prison, or captivity; freed from confinement.
examples: Sentenced to five years, he could be out in three with good behavior.

definitions.text: A radio procedure word meaning that the station is finished with its transmission and does not expect a response.
examples: Destruction. Two T-72s destroyed. Three foot mobiles down. Out.
Welp, I got nothing else to say. Mikey out.

definitions.text: Used as a brusque or dramatic farewell
examples: Welp, I got nothing else to say. Mikey out.
